title: calamus aureus
intro: Calamus aureus, also known as Golden Sweet Flag or Golden Calamus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acoraceae. It is a perennial aquatic plant with a long history of medicinal use, valued for its aromatic roots, which contain a volatile oil with analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Calamus aureus have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. The herbal preparations of Calamus aureus include teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used internally or externally to harness its health benefits.
